title: RIVER MUTANT
blurb: Williamsburg piers, low tide. You'll know it when you see it. The flame works. Wear the medal. Don't look at it after.
briefing: Where the East River cuts under the piers. The Pawnbroker has no name for this one. Flamethrower and a Saint-Anthony medal on a leather thong — wear the medal. The wooden icon nailed to the pylon has been worn smooth by current or something less neutral. Do what you came to do. Do not look at the body.
epigraph: "He pays you two thousand dollars in cash and says: there's one more."
